chief_complaint: A 27-year-old male patient presented with bilateral lower limb pain and dark brown urine after intense exercise.
history: The patient had no history of cardiac diseases. He developed severe chest pain, sweating, nausea, and vomiting during treatment.
exam: ECG was initially unremarkable but later indicated ST-T elevation in the V2, V3, V4, and V5 leads.
labs: Abnormal creatine kinase levels, urine tests suggested rhabdomyolysis, and elevated myocardial enzymes suggested acute myocardial infarction (AMI).
